steps.title: Identify what the claim needs
steps.content: The student's claim directly compares viewing times: people spend more time viewing art in museums than online. To support this comparison, evidence must provide actual time measurements from both locations.

steps.title: Find the choice with viewing-time evidence
steps.content: Choice A provides specific viewing times from both settings: three minutes at the museum versus one minute online. This directly measures the comparison the claim asserts. Choices B, C, and D address the work's online availability, visitor origins, and museum collections—none of which measure actual viewing time. That matches choice A — A is correct.
